title: penstemon payettensis
intro: Penstemon payettensis, also known as Payette penstemon, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Penstemon payettensis offer various health benefits, including soothing sore throats, reducing inflammation, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Penstemon payettensis can be prepared as a tea or a tincture for consumption, and its leaves and stems can also be used in topical applications.
